DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE STANDING TO PURSUE CLAIMS ON BEHALF OF THE FORMER OWNER OF THE PROPERTY DURING WHOSE OWNERSHIP THE CONTRACTUAL BREACHES AND TORTS HAVE OCCURRED (E.G. ASSIGNMENT OF CLAIMS). PLAINTIFF SHALL ALSO EXPRESSLY ALLEGE THAT THE CLAIMS SUBMITTED IN THE PRIOR ACTION ARE NOT THE SAME AS THE CLAIMS SUBMITTED IN THIS ACTION (E.G. DIFFERENT WINDOWS, DIFFERENT CHAIR, DIFFERENT RUG, ETC.). PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION SUBMITTED IN OPPOSITION TO THE DEMURRER IS STRICKEN IN ITS ENTIRETY. DEFENDANT'S DECLARATION IS STRICKEN EXCEPT FOR THE FIRST SENTENCE OF PARAGRAPH 7: "ON NOVEMBER 29, 2023, I HAD A MEET AND CONFER TELEPHONE CONFERENCE WITH MS. RISHINA CONCERNING THE 2SAC." REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS SANKAET PATHAK AND SYNAPSE FINANCIAL TECH, INC.'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COURT NOTES THAT THE OPPOSITION WAS FILED LATE WITHOUT LEAVE OF COURT AND WITHOUT ANY EXPLANATION AFTER 11:00 P.M. ON FRIDAY. WHILE THE OPPOSITION WILL BE CONSIDERED THIS TIME BECAUSE DEFENDANTS ADDRESSED IT ON THE MERITS IN THE REPLY, THE COURT WILL NOT CONSIDER ANY FILINGS IN VIOLATION OF THE PROCEDURAL RULES IN THE FUTURE. THIS ACTION WAS ORIGINALLY BROUGHT BY MILA POPOV, AN INDIVIDUAL. CONTRARY TO PLAINTIFF'S ARGUMENT IN OPPOSITION TO THE DEMURRER, THAT REFERENCES IN THE FAC'S PARAGRAPH 1 ARE SOLELY FOR CLARIFICATION PURPOSES, THE CAPTION OF THE FAC CLEARLY ADDS TWO MORE SEPARATELY NAMED PLAINTIFFS: "A TRUSTEE OF THE MILA POPOV LIVING TRUST;" AND "THE MANAGER OF SAKIMAN, LLC." IF POPOV DID NOT INTEND TO ADD TWO MORE PLAINTIFFS, POPOV MUST CLEARLY ALLEGE THAT SHE, AS AN INDIVIDUAL, IS THE ONLY PLAINTIFF BRINGING THIS ACTION. AS SUCH, SHE MUST ALLEGE, FACTS NOT CONCLUSIONS, SUPPORTING HER STANDING, AS AN INDIVIDUAL, TO BRING EACH CAUSE OF ACTION. IF THE INTENT WAS TO BRING THIS ACTION IN THE NAME OF OTHER PLAINTIFFS, ALLEGATIONS MUST BE MADE TO SHOW THAT THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS HAS NOT RUN AS TO THESE NEW PLAINTIFFS. REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. ANY AMENDMENT MADE TO THE CLAIMS REGARDING DAMAGES TO REAL AND PERSONAL PROPERTY MUST BE MADE IN GOOD FAITH AND MUST NOT OVERLAP WITH ANY CLAIMS REGARDING DAMAGES TO REAL AND PERSONAL PROPERTY ASSERTED IN THE PRIOR ACTION OR AN EXPLANATION MUST BE PROVIDED AS TO THE OVERLAP. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Off Calendar Dec-19-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY SANCTIONS SHOULD NOT BE IMPOSED/CONTEMPT

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: OFF CALENDAR. Counsel filed a Declaration on December 13, 2024, providing a satisfactory explanation for failure to comply with the Court's order re: personal appearance regarding a matter that could potentially involve an in-camera review. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)

DEFENDANT SANKAET PATHAK MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD is Off Calendar.

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-24-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT SANKAET PATHAK, SYNAPSE FINANCIAL TECH, INC. DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is OFF CALENDAR. Notice of Bankruptcy filing by one of the moving defendants, Synapse Financial Technologies, Inc, on file. The Court will not proceed on any aspect of this case involving Synapse Financial Technologies, Inc while bankruptcy is pending. The fully briefed motion by Synapse Financial Technologies, Inc may be re-set for hearing by an ex-parte application when bankruptcy is resolved.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-07-2024 Continued To Jul-09-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-30-2024 Continued To May-24-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT SANKAET PATHAK, SYNAPSE FINANCIAL TECH, INC. DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is continued to May 24, 2024. No later than May 15, 2024 Plaintiff to file a supplemental brief limited to citation to causes (no argument) involving similar facts re: subsequent real property owner's complaint as to causes of action accrued during the prior ownership. Plaintiff shall list up to 5 cases on point. If Plaintiff cannot locate any cases on point, Plaintiff shall state in the supplemental brief "No cases located." No further argument to be included.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-22-2024 Continued To May-21-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-30-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:

Svetlana Rishina Esq., Law offices of Svetlana Rishina, 510-299-5346, via zoom for plaintiff
Emily Charley Esq., Hanson Bridgett LLP, 415-777-3200, via zoom for defendants

Parties are not contesting to the tentative. Ms. Charley seeks clarification on 5 different points. Matter discussed. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SANKAET PATHAK, SYNAPSE FINANCIAL TECH, INC. DEMURRER TO 2ND Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege standing to pursue claims on behalf of the former owner of the property during whose ownership the contractual breaches and torts have occurred (e.g. assignment of claims). Plaintiff shall also expressly allege that the claims submitted in the prior action are not the same as the claims submitted in this action (e.g. different windows, different chair, different rug, etc.). Plaintiff's declaration submitted in opposition to the demurrer is stricken in its entirety. Defendant's declaration is stricken except for the first sentence of paragraph 7: "On November 29, 2023, I had a meet and confer telephone conference with Ms. Rishina concerning the 2SAC."

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)

Motion To Strike Improper, Irrelevant, And Confusing Matters
Off Calendar Jan-30-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:

Svetlana Rishina Esq., Law offices of Svetlana Rishina, 510-299-5346, via zoom for plaintiff
Emily Charley Esq., Hanson Bridgett LLP, 415-777-3200, via zoom for defendants

Parties are not contesting to the tentative. Ms. Charley seeks clarification on 5 different points. Matter discussed. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MILA POPOV MOTION TO STRIKE IMPROPER, IRRELEVANT, AND CONFUSING MATTERS is DENIED. Insufficient notice per CCP 1005. To the extent this motion was to serve as an objection to the defendant's request for judicial notice, the objection is overruled. The Court takes judicial notice of Court filings and attempted court filings submitted to the Court.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-29-2023 Continued To Jan-23-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-30-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANTS SANKAET PATHAK AND SYNAPSE FINANCIAL TECH, INC.'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend. The Court notes that the opposition was filed late without leave of Court and without any explanation after 11:00 p.m. on Friday. While the opposition will be considered this time because Defendants addressed it on the merits in the reply, the Court will not consider any filings in violation of the procedural rules in the future.
This action was originally brought by Mila Popov, an individual. Contrary to Plaintiff's argument in opposition to the demurrer, that references in the FAC's paragraph 1 are solely for clarification purposes, the caption of the FAC clearly adds two more separately named Plaintiffs: "a trustee of the Mila Popov Living Trust;" and "the manager of Sakiman, LLC." If Popov did not intend to add two more Plaintiffs, Popov must clearly allege that she, as an individual, is the only Plaintiff bringing this action. As such, she must allege, facts not conclusions, supporting her standing, as an individual, to bring each cause of action.
If the intent was to bring this action in the name of other plaintiffs, allegations must be made to show that the statute of limitations has not run as to these new plaintiffs.
Request for judicial notice is granted. Any amendment made to the claims regarding damages to real and personal property must be made in good faith and must not overlap with any claims regarding damages to real and personal property asserted in the prior action or an explanation must be provided as to the overlap.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
